name: boto3
summary: Official AWS SDK for Python — interact with S3, EC2, DynamoDB, Lambda, and 200+ AWS services.

story.problem: Your app needs to store user uploads in S3, send emails via SES, trigger a Lambda function, or read from DynamoDB. The AWS Console is for humans — you need to do this from code, with proper credentials, error handling, and pagination.
story.mental_model: Boto3 has two layers: resource (high-level, Pythonic objects like `s3.Bucket`) and client (low-level, 1:1 mapping to AWS API calls). Resources are friendlier for common tasks; clients give you full control. Both share the same credential chain (env vars → ~/.aws/credentials → IAM role).
story.when_to_use: Any Python code running against AWS services: uploading to S3, querying DynamoDB, invoking Lambda, sending SQS messages, managing EC2 instances.
story.when_not_to_use: Infrastructure provisioning — use CDK, Terraform, or CloudFormation instead. If you're on Google Cloud or Azure, use their respective SDKs.

alternatives.name: aiobotocore
alternatives.reason: Async wrapper around botocore for asyncio apps.

alternatives.name: s3fs
alternatives.reason: File-system interface over S3 — use it with pandas read_csv/to_csv on s3:// paths.

alternatives.name: AWS CDK
alternatives.reason: Infrastructure as code, not runtime API calls. Different tool.

nodes.label: boto3.client(service)
nodes.summary: Low-level client: `s3 = boto3.client('s3')`. All operations map 1:1 to AWS API. Returns raw dicts.

nodes.label: boto3.resource(service)
nodes.summary: High-level resource: `s3 = boto3.resource('s3')`. Pythonic objects (Bucket, Object, Table).

nodes.label: boto3.Session
nodes.summary: Manages credentials and region. Useful for switching profiles or assuming roles.

nodes.label: S3 operations
nodes.summary: upload_file(), download_file(), put_object(), get_object(), list_objects_v2(), generate_presigned_url().

nodes.label: Paginator
nodes.summary: Auto-paginate results: `paginator = client.get_paginator('list_objects_v2')`. Iterates all pages.

nodes.label: Waiter
nodes.summary: Poll until a condition is met: `ec2.get_waiter('instance_running').wait(InstanceIds=[id])`.
